'''Fonts''' tab in '''[[Toolkit menu|Toolkit]] : [[Options]]''' provides the following options: 

*'''Stylesheet''' opens the '''[[SuperMemo stylesheet|SuperMemo Stylesheet]]''' editor which makes it possible to define the default stylesheet for those [[HTML component|HTML components]], which do not use any stylesheet from the stylesheet [[Glossary:Registry|registry]]
*'''Use question and answer fonts in HTML''': This option, when checked, makes sure that each time you start editing HTML text, the fonts specified at '''Question font''' and '''Answer font''' are used by default. You can uncheck this box if you want to save space and leave HTML texts unformatted whenever possible (you can then use stylesheets or the Internet Explorer default setting to determine the default font used in [[HTML component|HTML components]]) 
*'''Interface font''' defines the font used in windows and dialog boxes throughout [[SuperMemo]]. To best way to see the impact of this option is to click the button and change the font. If you select a very large font, SuperMemo will ignore your choice so that you are able to read the dialogs and change your setting back. When an increase in the font size starts interfering with vital must-see information, the size of the font may be ignored and will be adjusted to make sure the text fits the allocated area (e.g. [[Glossary:collection|collection]] name and path on the navigation panel) (see: [https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=XL-6R955frc video]) 
*'''Question font''' defines the default font for texts [[Glossary:Component|components]]. This font will be used in all texts [[Glossary:Component|components]] where you do not specify a font yourself. Only answer [[Glossary:Component|components]] will use the font defined with '''Answer font''' below. Changing '''Question font''' will change the initial font used in editing HTML or RTF [[Glossary:Component|components]] (if the following option above is checked: '''Use question and answer fonts in HTML'''). It will also affect '''all''' plain text [[Glossary:Component|components]] whose font is not specified. It will not change the appearance of HTML and RTF [[Glossary:Component|components]] created in the past. Whenever SuperMemo is not sure which font to use, it will use '''Question font''' in various circumstances 
*'''Answer font''' defines the default font used in texts [[Glossary:Component|components]] serving as answers to questions. It will be used for all plain text answers unless you specifically request another font be used. If you change '''Answer font''', answers of plain text [[Glossary:Component|components]] that do not specify the font will also change throughout your [[Glossary:Collection|collection]]. This will not affect answers edited as HTML or RTF 
*'''Contents font''' defines the default font used in the [[Contents|'''Contents''' window]]. This font will also be used to display element titles in [[Glossary:Registry|registries]]
*'''Highlight font''' defines the font used to highlight texts in [[Glossary:Incremental_reading|incremental reading]] (e.g. with the highlighter icon on the [[Read toolbar|'''Read''' toolbar]]). This font may be overridden by the font used in the stylesheet applied to a given [[HTML component]]

<div class="center">
[[Image:Options fonts.jpg|800px|SuperMemo: Toolkit : Options : Fonts tab]] 
<blockquote>'''''Figure''': Changing the interface font in SuperMemo''</blockquote>
</div>

There are four basic font options on the '''[[Component menu#Text|Text]]''' submenu of the [[component menu]] in all text [[Glossary:Component|components]]. 

*'''New font''' makes it possible to define a new font, add it to the font [[Glossary:Registry|registry]] for later use (see: '''[[Search menu|Search]] : [[Search menu#Other registries|Other registries]] : Font'''). After the new font is defined it is applied to the current selection in HTML and RTF [[Glossary:Component|components]] or to the whole text in plain text components 
*'''Link font''' (''Shift+Ctrl+F'') can be used to reuse a font previously stored in the font [[Glossary:Registry|registry]]
*'''Edit font''' allows you to edit the currently used font in a text [[Glossary:Component|component]]. In HTML and RTF components, the modified font will only affect the current selection. In plain text [[Glossary:Component|components]]], the font in the entire text will change. In addition, the font in the currently used [[Glossary:Template|template]] will be modified 
*'''Default font''' can be used in plain text [[Glossary:Component|components]] to detach a registry font from the current component and the current [[Glossary:Template|template]] (if any). This, for example, will make the modified template use '''Question font''' and '''Answer font''' as defined in '''[[Toolkit menu|Toolkit]] : [[Options]] : Fonts''' (see above). This option is of little use in [[HTML component|HTML components]], which have their font settings overriden by stylesheets and HTML tags

RTF and HTML [[Glossary:Component|components]] can use either formatted text with many fonts or space-saving plain text. To convert the text in HTML or RTF [[Glossary:Component|components]] to plain text use '''[[Component menu#Text|Text]] : Convert : Plain text''' on the [[component menu]] (e.g. press ''Ctrl+Shift+F12''). To change the default font used in unformatted plain text in your [[HTML component|HTML components]], change the stylesheet used in the [[Glossary:Component|component]], or, if the stylesheet does not define the font, the setting in your Internet Explorer ('''Tools : Internet Options : Fonts'''). Once you modify fonts in an unformatted text in [[HTML component|HTML components]], the text will be saved as HTML again. 

== See also ==

*[[Using fonts|Fonts in SuperMemo]] 
*[https://supermemopedia.com/wiki/Finding_out_the_default_font_size_used_in_HTML_components FAQ: How to determine which font is used?]
